description: You can execute these commands from the CLI, as part of an automation, or in a playbook.
examples:
Command Example
#
!cymptom-get-mitigations
Human Readable Output
#
Mitigations
#
ID

!cymptom-get-mitigations
Human Readable Output
#
Mitigations
#
ID
Name
Severity Type

Command Example
#
!cymptom-get-users-with-cracked-passwords privileged=False
Human Readable Output
#
Unprivileged Users With Cracked Passwords
#
Username

!cymptom-get-users-with-cracked-passwords privileged=False
Human Readable Output
#
Unprivileged Users With Cracked Passwords
#
Username
user1
user2

outputs.description: The severity of the mitigation Cymptom.Mitigations.Name The name of the mitigation
outputs.name: Cymptom.Mitigations.SeverityType

outputs.description: The percentage of attack vectors used that can be mitigated Cymptom.Mitigations.ID The mitigation's ID
outputs.name: Cymptom.Mitigations.AttackVectorsUsedPercentage

outputs.description: The attack vectors counts that can be mitigated Cymptom.Mitigations.Techniques Techniques relevant for this mitigation
outputs.name: Cymptom.Mitigations.AttackVectorsCount

outputs.description: Username of users with cracked passwords Context Example
outputs.name: Cymptom.CrackedUsers.Username
